title: Sub-select in LIMIT clause may access db without locks.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(255), value DOUBLE PRECISION, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, value) VALUES ('Bob', NULL);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL LIMIT 1);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE value = (SELECT MAX(value) FROM test_table WHERE name LIKE 'A%') LIMIT 1;
key_bug_features: Sub-select in LIMIT clause without locks
